items.hook: ‘At times I felt I’d bitten off more than I could chew’: Christopher Nolan on sweeping the Oscars, making The Odyssey – and getting a puppy
items.body:
How do you follow Oppenheimer? By spending $250m bringing Homer’s epic poem to the big screen in Imax. Today’s most powerful director talks big swings, trauma-bonding and the healing powers of chocolate labrador Charlie

items.hook: Five arrested in Hong Kong bookstore raids in ‘seditious’ materials crackdown
items.body:
Third round of arrests linked to independent bookshops widely regarded as clampdown on dissent in territory

Hong Kong authorities have raided two bookstores and arrested five people on suspicion of selling allegedly seditious publi

items.hook: Heartstopper Forever review – sanitized sex scenes won’t let the Netflix lovebirds grow up
items.body:
The film-length finale to the teen LGBTQ+ show has poignant moments but feels like fan service by numbers

If it were up to Kit Connor, Heartstopper would have ended

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: The Secret Life of Bees is a stage musical with a book by Lynn Nottage, music by Duncan Sheik and lyrics by Susan Birkenhead.
items.body: The musical premiered Off-Broadway in 2019 to critical acclaim, winning the Outer Critics Circle Award for Outstanding New Off-Broadway Musical, along with Best New Musical nominations from the Drama Desk Awards, Lucille Lortel Awards, and the Drama League Awa

items.hook: ‘Some fantastic mischief lurking just around the grin’: Sam Neill by Tara Fitzgerald – a poem
items.body:
Neill’s co-star in the 1994 comedy-drama Sirens remembers a man of rare beauty, generosity and delight

By the time I met Sam
He had already assumed a kind of mythic status in our household,
playing Reilly on Ace of S

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: Camilla Trinchieri is an Italian-American mystery writer and novelist.
items.body: She has published under three names: Camilla Trinchieri, Trella Crespi, and Camilla T. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Captain Antifer is an adventure novel written by Jules Verne.
items.body: The novel tells of a treasure hunt, where the clues – arriving in bits and pieces – lead the seaman Pierre Antife of Saint-Malo and various others of diverse nationalities and backgrounds on a complicated route – from Tunisia to the Persian Gulf, the Gulf of G

items.hook: Haunted People is a 1932 German-Czech drama film directed by Friedrich Feher and starring Magda Sonja, Hans Feher, and Eugen Klöpfer.
items.body: It was shot at the Staaken Studios in Berlin and on location in Marseille. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The B.E.4 and B.E.7 were virtually identical aircraft that differed only in the engine fitted.
items.body: The Royal Aircraft Factory B.E.3 was a single-engined rotary engined biplane developed by the British Royal Aircraft Factory prior to the First World War. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Former emperor Peter III of Russia is murdered.
items.body: Peter III Fyodorovich was Emperor of Russia from 5 January 1762 until 9 July of the same year, when his wife, Catherine II, overthrew him in a palace coup d'état. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The 7.0 Mw Papua New Guinea earthquake triggers a tsunami that destroys ten villages in Papua New Guinea, killing up to 2,700 people, and leaving several thousand injured.
items.body: The 1998 Papua New Guinea earthquake occurred on July 17 with a moment magnitude of 7.0 and a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Andrew Motion: ‘Wilfred Owen became a kind of sacred text for me’
items.body:
The former poet laureate on growing up with Lawrence Durrell, rereading Henry James and getting to grips with the genius of Alexander Pope

My earliest reading memory
My parents were country people who thought t

items.hook: Slippy McGee is a 1923 American silent drama film directed by Wesley Ruggles and based on the book Slippy McGee: Sometimes Known as the Butterfly Man by Marie Conway Oemler that was published in 1917.
items.body: The film was an Oliver Morosco Production released by Associated First National and featured actress Colleen Moore as Mary Virginia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Nuclear weapons testing: The "Small Boy" test shot Little Feller I becomes the last atmospheric test detonation at the Nevada National Security Site.
items.body: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: After takeoff from Husein Sastranegara International Airport, Sempati Air Flight 304 crashes into a residential neighborhood in Bandung, killing 28 people.
items.body: Husein Sastranegara Airport is a domestic airport serving Bandung, the capital of West Java, Indonesia. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Interview: Inna Sahakyan, Ruben Ghazaryan • Directors of Outliving Shakespeare - “Older people are often the most open to life” - Golden Apricot 2026
items.body: The Armenian duo reveal details about the making of their heartfelt documentary, as well as their differing approaches to finding the right balance, both on set and in the editing room

items.hook: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, a Boeing 777, crashes near the border of Ukraine and Russia after being shot down. All 298 people on board are killed.
items.body: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17 was a scheduled passenger flight from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur that was shot down by Russian-backed forces with a Buk 9M38 surface-to-air missile on 17 July 2014, while flying over eastern Ukraine. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: TAM Airlines Flight 3054, an Airbus A320, crashes into a warehouse after landing too fast and missing the end of the São Paulo–Congonhas Airport runway, killing 199 people.
items.body: TAM Airlines Flight 3054 was a regularly scheduled domestic passenger flight operated by TAM Airlines from Porto Alegre to São Paulo, Brazil. The world is stranger than it lets on.
